# Oxidase mimicking of CuMnO2nanoflowers and the application in colorimetric detection of ascorbic acid


Qi Lian a,b,1 , Xuefang Zheng b,c,*,1 , Guirong Peng a,** , Zhanquan Liu b , Li Chen b , Shu Wu d

- b College of Chemical Engineering, Hebei Normal University of Science and Technology, 360West Section of Hebei Street, Qinhuangdao066004, PR China

- c School of Chemical Engineering and Technology, Hebei University of Technology, 8Guangrong Road, Hongqiao District, Tianjin300130, PR China

- d Department of Ecology, Hebei University of Environmental Engineering, 8Jingang Avenue, Beidaihe District, Qinhuangdao066102, PR China


# H I G H L I G H T S


- CuMnO2nanoflowers were synthesized.

- CuMnO2nanoflowers exhibited enhanced oxidase-like catalytic activity.

- The  colorimetric  CuMnO2nanoflowers sensor hold great potential for detection of AA.


# A R T I C L E  I N F O


Keywords: CuMnO2nanoflowers Ascorbic acid TMB Colorimetric assay Oxidase-like enzyme Nanozymes


# G R A P H I C A L  A B S T R A C T



# A B S T R A C T


As a novel artificial enzyme, CuMnO2nanoflowers (CuMnO2NFs) were synthesized by a hydrothermal reaction, and the morphology could be obtained by adjusting the temperature and time of reaction. CuMnO2NFs was a new type of artificial enzyme with oxidase-like properties. Compared with traditional copper-based artificial enzymes, CuMnO2NFs were simple to prepare and did not require the assistance of other active substances. Due to the large number of active sites on the surface of CuMnO2NFs and the synergistic reaction of Cu + /Cu2 + and Mn3 + /Mn4 + redox couples in CuMnO2NFs, CuMnO2NFs could catalyze3,3 ´ ,5,5 ´ -tetramethylbenzidine (TMB, colorless) to oxidized TMB (ox-TMB, blue) without hydrogen peroxide. When ascorbic acid (AA) bound to oxTMB, ox-TMB (blue) was reduced to TMB (colorless). Based on the oxidase-like ability of CuMnO2NFs, the reaction system composed of CuMnO2NFs and TMB had the ability to detect AA content with a large detection range of1 -105 µ M and the limit of detection (LOD) was0.39 µ M. This assay could detect the AA concentration in fruit and vegetable juices effectively. The spiked experimental results of TMB-CuMnO2NFs system showed that

# 1. Introduction


In recent years, due to the shortcomings of biological enzymes in terms of temperature adaptability and economy, nanozymes have made great progress as a new type of artificial enzymes. Nanozymes exhibit excellent performance in temperature adaptability, preparation method, catalytic  efficiency,  catalytic  stability,  and  economical  availability [1 -6]. Various sensors based on nanozyme technology have been used in many  fields,  including:  enzyme  catalysis  [7],  biosensors  [8],  water quality detection [9], electrochemical sensors [10], colorimetric sensors [11],  fluorescence  sensors  [12],  environmental  monitoring  [13]  and food composition analysis [14]. In order to expand the application scope and  improve  the  catalytic  performance  of  nanozymes,  various  nanozymes with different properties have been developed, including: metal oxides  [15 -23],  metal  sulfides  [24],  noble  metals  [25 -29],  metal organic frameworks (MOF) [30], carbon nanomaterials [31 -33], and nanometal  composites  [34 -36].  The  nanozymes  exhibited  excellent oxidase-like, peroxidase-like, superoxide dismutase-like and catalase-like properties.

As an important type of nanozymes, manganese oxide nanoparticles have  attracted  much  attention  in  recent  years  due  to  their  excellent enzyme mimicking properties. For example, xuefang zheng synthesized sea urchin-like manganese tetroxide (Mn3O4) nanoparticles by a simple and mild hydrothermal method, which could be combined with TMB to form a colorimetric detection sensor for glutathione (GSH) detection [37]. E. L. Ding fabricated MnCo2O4nanotubes by2D MnCo2O4nanosheets, and developed a pressure and colorimetric bifunctional sensor for the detection of cancer cells in serum samples [38]. By changing the amount of branched polyethyleneimine (BPEI), linear MnO2, tubular octahedral  Mn3O4nanoparticles  were  successfully  synthesized,  all  of which had excellent oxidase-like properties and could be used to detect glutathione [39]. A zirconium-based coordination polymer and MnO2nanoneedles  were  cross-linked  to  form  hollow  microspheres,  which were used for protein immobilization [40]. The LaMnO3 + δ nanofibers with ABO3-type perovskite structure prepared by calcination had efficient oxidase-like catalytic activity, and a colorimetric detection method for L-cysteine was established based on this material [41]. Manganese oxide and manganese oxide composite with various morphologies had been  widely  reported  in  the  direction  of  artificial  nanozymes  and applied  to  the  detection  of  ascorbic  acid  (AA),  glutathione  (GSH), glucose  and  DNA  [42 -53].  Various  Mn-based  artificial  enzymes  had excellent intrinsic oxidase-like properties. Nevertheless, the preparation process of most manganese nanozyme was cumbersome and the preparation temperature was high relatively. The preparation of Mn-based artificial enzyme in a facile and mild way remains the focus of attention.

Copper oxides and their composites were also important components of nanozymes. For instance, by using bovine serum albumin (BSA) as a cross-linking agent, Pt nanoparticles were grown on CuO nanoflowers to form  CuO/Pt  nanoflowers  with  excellent  peroxidase-like  properties. CuO/Pt nanoflowers could be combined with TMB to form a sensor for detecting Hg2 + in water [9]. CuS nanoparticles were grown on sheet graphene  to  form  CuS-graphene  nanosheets,  which  had  excellent peroxidase-like properties. CuS-graphene nanosheets could realize the quantitative  detection  of  hydrogen  peroxide  in  the  presence  of  TMB [54]. In recent years, various Cu-based artificial enzyme with special nanostructures  have  been  developed,  such  as  Polyacrylonitrile-CuO [11],  Graphene@CuO/WO3 [55],  Cu0.89Zn0.11O  [56],  Ag3PO4/CuO [57],  CuO@rGO  [58],  Cu(OH)2 [19],  PtPdCu  [59],  Cu@Au  [60], Cu2GLYPH<0> xSe  [61].  They  all  had  intrinsic  peroxidase-like  properties  and achieved remarkable achievements in trace element detection, the recoveries were between95.1% and104.8%, and the RSDs were no more than2.87%. Comparison with the HPLC method, the TMB-CuMnO2NFs colorimetric detection method was effective and accurate for the detection of AA in fruit and vegetable juices.

environmental monitoring, cancer therapy and biosensors. Copper oxides and their composites had the characteristics of simple preparation process and mild preparation conditions.

Copper compounds and various complexes have excellent peroxidase-like properties, but the peroxidase-like properties of copper compounds must involve the participation of hydrogen peroxide, which makes the application process cumbersome. In contrast, the oxidase-like catalytic process did not require the participation of hydrogen peroxide, and the detection process was simple. In order to endow copper with oxidase-like properties, copper could be combined with elements which had  intrinsic  oxidase-like  properties  to  form  nanocage  (Co3O4/CuO hollow nanocage) [62], copper ions could form complexes with carbon elements (Cu-CDs, Cu-OC, Cu2O-CDs-Cu, Cu3/ND@G) [63 -67], copper ions formed complexes, and copper ions were complexed with biological macromolecules [68]. At present, most of the methods for imparting oxidase-like properties of copper had complicated reaction processes. The prepared products had complex structure and unsatisfactory environmental  adaptability.  Manganese  compounds,  as  substances  with intrinsic oxidase-like properties, could be combined with copper to form CuMnO2nanoflowers which had the advantages of two elements. At the same time, CuMnO2nanoflower, as a single inorganic compound, had the  characteristics  of  stable  performance,  simple  structure  and  easy preparation process.

Ascorbic acid (AA) is an important substance to maintain the normal function of the human body. Insufficient intake of AA in the human body is  inextricably  linked  with the  occurrence of cardiovascular diseases, neurological diseases, and tumor diseases [69 -71]. However, due to the lack of necessary biological enzymes, human cannot synthesize AA to maintain their own activities, the AA required by the human body needs to be obtained from food to satisfy the normal operation of human body functions [72,73]. Rapid and accurate detection of AA content in food can play an important role in addressing the issue of ensuring adequate daily AA intake. In recent years, a series of detection methods for AA content had been invented, including: electrochemistry [74,75], chemiluminescence [76], high-performance liquid chromatography [77,78], fluorescence  [79].  Among  many  detection  methods,  the  colorimetric assay has attracted extensive attention due to its low detection cost, simple  detection  procedure,  and  no  need  for  complex  equipment support.

Enlightened by the above facts, we synthesized CuMnO2nanoflowers (NFs) by a hydrothermal method. Due to the synergistic effect of Cu and Mn elements and the numbers active sites on the petals, CuMnO2NFs showed  enhanced  oxidase-like  performance.  When  CuMnO2NFs encountered the chromogenic substrate TMB, the colorless TMB was oxidized  by  CuMnO2NFs  to  form  blue  ox-TMB  in  the  absence  of hydrogen peroxide. After AA was added to the reaction system, ox-TMB could be reduced to TMB and the color of the solution was faded from blue gradually. The color changes could be observed by the naked eye directly.  Therefore,  TMB and CuMnO2NFs could constitute a colorimetric sensor for detecting AA which could be applied to the measurement of AA content in fruit and vegetable juices.

The preparation of CuMnO2NFs by a simple hydrothermal reaction endowed copper compounds with oxidase-like properties and greatly improved  the  performance  of  copper-based  artificial  enzymes  whose main  intrinsic  properties  were  peroxidase-like  properties  [9,11,16, 54 -56]. CuMnO2NFs improved the application of copper compounds in the  direction  of  artificial  enzymes.  Compared  with  the  few  existing methods that endowed copper compounds with oxidase-like properties [62 -68], the preparation method of CuMnO2NFs was simple, did not require the assistance of various organic chemical, and did not require

the support of various auxiliary reactions. CuMnO2NFs expanded the application scenarios of Cu-based artificial enzymes, which was a new expansion in the field of artificial enzymes.


# 2. Experimental



# 2.1. Preparation of CuMnO2nanoflowers (CuMnO2NFs)


400mg MnSO4 ⋅ H2O and1g KMnO4were dissolved in40mL ultrapure water. The solution was stirred at20 ◦ C for20min to dissolve MnSO4 ⋅ H2O  and  KMnO4in  ultrapure  water  completely.  690mg CuSO40.5H2O was fully dissolved in MnSO4-KMnO4solution. Then, the reaction precursor solution was transferred to a100mL hydrothermal reactor and reacted at100 ◦ C for10h, and the heating rate during this process was controlled to be10 ◦ C per minute. After the reaction was completed, the hydrothermal reactor was rapidly cooled by an ice bath, so as to prompt the rapid termination of the reaction and prevent the continuous reaction from generating larger crystals. The reaction solution was centrifuged at100rpm and washed four times with ultrapure water. The product was dried at30 ◦ C to obtain CuMnO2nanoflowers.


# 2.2. Oxidase-like activity of CuMnO2NFs


The oxidase-like activity of CuMnO2nanoflowers was investigated by the catalytic oxidation of TMB. Briefly, 200 μ L of CuMnO2dispersion (65 μ g) and300 μ L TMB (0.4mM in ethanol) solution were added to acetate buffer solution (pH4.0) with a final volume of500 μ L at30 ◦ C. The transparent TMB would be catalyzed to form blue oxidized TMB (oxTMB) for the oxidase-like performance of CuMnO2. The bluer the color of the oxTMB, the stronger oxidase-like capability of the catalyst. Oxidase-like performance could be monitored by an ultraviolet spectrophotometer at652nm.


# 2.3. Selection of optimal reaction conditions and kinetic analysis


Analysis of the different reaction conditions of CuMnO2NFs (60 ◦ C140 ◦ C, 8 -12h), the CuMnO2NFs concentration (5 -70 µ g/mL), TMB (0 -0.5mM), pH (2GLYPH<0> 9) and temperature (10 -50 ◦ C) on the enzyme activity of oxidase. Under optimal conditions, the reaction kinetics were performed  by  varying  the  TMB  content  (0 -0.5mM)  and  the  kinetic parameters were determined in accordance with the Michaelis-Menten equation:

The above equation reveals the influence of enzyme and substrate content on the rate of catalytic reaction in an enzyme catalyzed reaction. Where V represents the initial velocity of the substrate conversion, Vmax stands the maximal reaction velocity, [S] is the concentration of substrate (TMB), Km is the Michaelis constant. Further, the values of the kinetic  parameters Vmax and Km could  be  obtained  by  the  following Lineweaver-Burk plot.


# 2.4. Colorimetric assay for AA


The important basis of the colorimetric detection method for AA was that the AA concentration in the TMB-CuMnO2NFs catalytic system had the excellent color linear change at652nm, which was based on the splendid oxidase-like performance of CuMnO2NFs. With the increase of AA concentration, the TMB-CuMnO2NFs reaction system turned from ultramarine  (ox  TMB)  to  colorless  (TMB)  gradually.  The  optimal detection environment for colorimetric assay was as follows: 65 µ g/mL CuMnO2NFs, 0.4mM TMB, 0 -150 µ M AA, pH4.0and the colorimetric detection time of2min at30 ◦ C. The absorbance value of the solution at652nm would show a regular linear change with the change of AA concentration. According to this, the relationship between the AA concentration and the absorbance value was established. The detection of AA was executed under the optimal detection environment.

Simultaneously, to verify the specificity of the oxidase-like reaction for  AA,  the  TMB-CuMnO2NFs was applied to different interferences including  uric  acid,  adipic  acid,  KI,  tartaric  acid,  dopamine,  benzoic acid, glucose, oxalic acid, cinnamic acid, KBr and citric acid.


# 2.5. Analysis of the AA in fruit and vegetable juices

# 3. Results and discussion



# 3.1. Preparation and characterization of CuMnO2nanoflowers


In order to appraise the effect of reaction conditions on the CuMnO2nanoflower oxidase-like performance capability, the influence of reaction temperature (60 ◦ C, 80 ◦ C, 100 ◦ C, 120 ◦ C and140 ◦ C) and reaction time (8h, 10h, 12h at100 ◦ C) on the surface morphology of CuMnO2NFs was investigated by SEM (Figs. 1, S1, S2). Meanwhile, BET (Fig. 2 (A), S3) was also used to survey the CuMnO2NFs specific surface area produced under different reaction conditions. The size and morphology of the CuMnO2NFs under optimal reaction conditions were investigated by SEM. The SEM (Fig. S1) showed that the average diameter of the

CuMnO2NFs increased from80nm to3 µ m with the reaction temperature from80 ◦ C to140 ◦ C. When the reaction temperature was60 ◦ C and80 ◦ C, CuMnO2existed in the form of nanospheres (Fig. S1 (A), (B)). When the reaction temperature rose to100 ◦ C, the CuMnO2as the final products presented  a  beautiful  petal-like  structure  of  flower  (Fig.  S1 (C)). However, when the reaction temperature went up to120 ◦ C and140 ◦ C,  the  size  of  CuMnO2continued  to  increase  to  form  a  block structure (Fig. S1 (D), (E)). It revealed that the CuMnO2NFs produced at100 ◦ C had excellent petal-like structure. The morphologies of CuMnO2NFs under different reaction time at100 ◦ C were investigated by SEM (Fig. S2). The SEM (Fig. S2) indicated that the CuMnO2NFs had a good petal-like structure when the reaction time was10h at100 ◦ C. The BET (Figs.  2  (A),  S3(D))  of  CuMnO2NFs  manufactured in10h at100 ◦ C displayed excellent nitrogen adsorption performance with the largest specific surface area (443.5m2 /g) compared with goods produced under other reaction conditions. Pore size distribution (Inset into Fig. S3 (D)) exhibited that the pore diameters were  mainly  concentrated  at10 -30nm  when  the  CuMnO2NFs  generated  under100 ◦ C  for10h. Correspondingly, the pore size was in the range of5 -15nm when the CuMnO2NFs were produced under other conditions. After the petal-like structure was formed, the surface structures of CuMnO2were mainly nano-petal-like structures and nano-holes. A large number of petal-like structures increased the specific surface area of the particles, and the attraction  to  the  reaction  substrate  was  greatly  enhanced.  The  large number of nanopores formed on CuMnO2NFs provided a stable reaction site for the oxidase-like reaction, which was beneficial to the coordinated reaction of Cu and Mn. # 3.2. Oxidase-like activity of CuMnO2NFs


The outstanding oxidase-like properties of CuMnO2NFs were manifested in that CuMnO2NFs could catalyze TMB efficiently without the participation  of  hydrogen  peroxide,  which  was  a  typical  feature  of oxidase-like substrates. Recent evidence suggested that oxygen had a certain influence on the oxidase-like reaction, and the change of the external environment of the reaction system (O2-, air-, and N2-saturated) had an impact on the result of the oxidase-like reaction [37]. The results of  the  catalytic  reaction  of  CuMnO2NFs  on  TMB  (from  colorless  to mazarine) under different external reaction conditions (O2-, air-, and N2-saturated) were compared in Fig. 4 (A). The absorbance of TMB were all detected at652nm in all reaction systems. The dark blue solution appeared in the O2-saturated reaction system which showed the highest absorption  in  the  UV  spectrophotometer.  By  contrast,  the  light  blue solution occurred in the nitrogen-saturated reaction system which displayed the lowest absorption. When the external environment of the reaction system was air medium, the adsorption intensity of TMB was between O2and N2medium which revealed a moderate-intensity blue. The result assumed that the oxygen content of the external environment had considerable impact on the final results of the oxidase-like reaction.

H2O2was a common reagent in TMB catalyzed reaction [5,9]. Fig. 4 (B) showed the effect of H2O2content on the CuMnO2NFs catalytic reaction. Comparing curve a and b (Fig. 4 (B)), the UV absorption curve of CuMnO2NFs solution containing TMB (curve b) displayed an obvious absorption  peak  at652nm,  which  indicated  that  CuMnO2NFs  had excellent oxidase-like performance in the presence of TMB. After adding H2O2to CuMnO2NFs and TMB, the UV adsorption curve (curve c in Fig. 4 (B)) was slightly improved at652nm, which revealed that H2O2was inappreciable to the oxidase-like activity of CuMnO2and TMB. That might be due to the synergistic effect of CuMnO2NFs and the large specific surface area brought by the petal-like structure of CuMnO2NFs.

In order to prove the reaction mechanism of the catalytic behavior of CuMnO2NFs, it was important to explain that the decomposition of molecular oxygen catalyzed by CuMnO2NFs to generate reactive oxygen species (ROS). The copper and manganese were common catalysts in the oxidase-like reactions which reacted with oxygen to produce superoxide radicals  (O2 · -).  The  CuMnO2NFs possessed the oxidation states Cu + / Cu2 + and Mn2 + /Mn3 + . Frist of all, O2directly got an electron from the Cu + and Mn3 + , making O2turned into O2 · -. At the same time, the Cu + was oxidized into Cu2 + , and Mn3 + was changed into Mn4 + (Eqs. (1) -(2)). Then the O2 · -combined with H + to form H2O2and O2 (Eq. (3)). Simultaneously, the hydrogen peroxide oxidized Cu + and Mn3 + to form Cu2 + and Mn4 + , while the H2O2obtained electrons to become OH -and OH · (Eqs. (4) -(5)). Finally, Cu2 + and Mn4 + deprived the electrons from TMB to form Cu + and Mn3 + , while the TMB was transformed into ox-TMB which promoted the reaction system into blue color (Eqs. (6) -(7)) [80, 90].

The mechanism study of the oxidase-like reaction of CuMnO2NFs clarified the role of O2 · -and OH · in the catalytic reaction. There was evidence  that  O2 · -played  a  crucial  role  in  the  oxidase-like  reaction. Correspondingly, the OH · content was maintained at a low level relatively  during  the  whole  reaction  process,  which  indicated  that  OH · played a weak auxiliary role in the catalytic reaction process. Both O2 · -and OH · were derived from dissolved oxygen which attached to the petal-like structures of CuMnO2NFs. Therefore, the petal-like structure of CuMnO2NFs was a key instrument for the oxidase-like reaction and the possible mechanism was summarized in Scheme1.

With large specific surface area and numerous nanolayered holes, petal-shaped CuMnO2NFs could provide a large number of active sites for oxidase-like reaction which could not only fix the reactant on the surface  of  the  nanoflower  but  also  promote  the  transfer  of  electrons between  TMB  and  ox-TMB  [11].  Meanwhile,  the  Cu + /Cu2 + and

Mn3 + /Mn4 + redox couples provided by CuMnO2NFs had an excellent synergistic effect, which could improve the oxidase-like reaction effectively [94].


# 3.3. Optimal environment for the oxidase-like reaction


The smooth and effective progress of the oxidase-like reaction by the CuMnO2NFs was closely related to the pH of the reaction environment, the content of TMB, the concentration of CuMnO2NFs and the reaction time. The effect of pH values on oxidase-like reaction was shown on the Fig. 5 (A). The pH of the reaction environment could affect the content of H2O2and O2in the reaction system (Eq. (3)), as well as the synergistic effect between Cu + /Cu2 + and Mn3 + /Mn4 + (Eqs. (4) and (5)). When the pH of the oxidase-like reaction was4.0 (Fig. 5 (A)), the absorbance was maximum at the wavelength of652nm, which indicated that the content of oxTMB in the system was highest and the oxidase-like reaction process  was completed.  The  Fig.  5revealed  the  optimal  oxidase-like reaction  condition:  the  reaction  temperature  was30 ◦ C  (Fig.  5  (B)), the  content  of  TMB  was0.4mM  (Fig.  5  (C)),  the  concentration  of CuMnO2NFs was65 µ g/mL (Fig. 5 (D)), and the catalytic reaction time was2.0min (Fig. 5 (E)).

The stability of oxidase-like reaction for the CuMnO2NFs was tested in Fig. 6. Under the optimal reaction conditions, the absorbance value of oxTMB was not changed significantly within30days, which indicated that the state of oxTMB catalyzed by CuMnO2NFs was stable (Fig. 6). Meanwhile,  the  absorbance  value  of  TMB  reduced  by  AA  remained stable within30days, suggesting that oxTMB was reduced by AA to form stable TMB.

# 3.5. Colorimetric detection of AA


CuMnO2NFs  had  excellent  oxidase-like  properties,  which  could catalyze TMB (colorless) to form oxidized TMB (blue). AA is a substance with  strong  reducing  ability.  When  AA  contacted  oxidized  TMB,  the oxidized TMB (blue) was reduced to TMB (colorless). The color change process  could  be  observed  at652nm  quantitatively.  Therefore,  the concentration of AA formed a linear relationship with the absorbance value of the solution at652nm, and the concentration of AA in the solution could be confirmed by reading the absorbance value. Meanwhile, the color of the TMB-CuMnO2NFs reaction system also changed with the content of AA. The detection of AA was carried out under the optimal detection environment (pH4.0, reaction temperature: 30 ◦ C, reaction time: 2min, CuMnO2NFs: 65 µ g/mL, TMB: 0.4mM). As shown in Fig. 8, when the solubility of AA in the solution increased from0 µ M to150 µ M, the absorbance value of the solution decreased at652nm gradually (Fig. 8 (A), (B)). Correspondingly, the color of the solution changed from mazarine blue to colorless gradually (inset, Fig. 8 (A)). Within a certain range of the content of AA (1 -105 µ M), the absorbance value of TMB had an excellent linear relationship with the concentration of  AA  (Fig.  8  (C))  and  the  LOD  was0.39 µ M.  The  linear  regression equation was shown in Fig. 8 (C) and Abs = 1.0972 -0.0099[HQ] (R2 = 0.9995). This illustrated that the colorimetric reaction system composed of CuMnO2NFs and TMB could be applied to the detection of AA concentration. Table2summarized the detection range and LOD of the proposed artificial enzymes for substrates. Compared with Cu-based artificial  enzymes  (CuO,  CuO-Pt,  CuNCs),  the  detection  range  of CuMnO2NFs was1 -105 µ M, which was favorable for the detection of low concentrations of AA. The LOD of CuMnO2NFs was also significantly  lower  than  that  of  the  proposed  Cu-based  artificial  enzymes, which expanded the application range of Cu-based artificial enzymes. Compared with the proposed Mn-based artificial enzymes, CuMnO2NFs had a wider linear measurement range, which made up for the narrow measurement range of MnO2. Compared with other artificial enzymes, CuMnO2NFs had advantages in linear range and LOD.

The selection specificity of the TMB-CuMnO2NFs system sensor was displayed in Fig. 8 (D). Different reducing agents such as AA, uric acid, adipic  acid,  KI,  tartaric  acid,  dopamine,  benzoic  acid,  benzoic  acid, glucose, oxalic acid, cinnamic acid, KBr, and citric acid were added into the  TMB-CuMnO2NFs  reaction  system  at  a  certain  concentration (10mM), and the absorbance of the system was detected at652nm. Only  in  the  TMB-CuMnO2reaction  system  where  AA  (100 µ M)  was added, the absorbance value changed significantly at652nm. Correspondingly, even if other detected substances were added to the reaction system with a much higher concentration (10mM) than that of AA, the absorbance value of reaction system only changed slightly at652nm. The results indicated that the TMB-CuMnO2NFs demonstrated a high degree of selectivity and effectiveness for AA.

The illustration of the catalytic reaction process and TMB-CuMnO2NFs  detection  mechanism  was  shown  in  Scheme2.  As  a  common oxidase-like  substrate,  TMB  would  form  ox-TMB  (blue)  from  TMB (colorless)  when  it  encountered  oxidase-like  enzymes.  After  adding CuMnO2NFs into the TMB solution, the TMB solution (colorless) was turned into ox-TMB (blue) within2min which was mainly attributed to the attack on TMB by the massive negative charges generated under the catalysis of CuMnO2NFs. In the meantime, the absorbance value of the TMB-CuMnO2solution increased dramatically at652nm. Moreover, the absorbance value at652nm could remain stable for a long time, and did not change due to environmental changes, which ensured the accuracy of the experimental results. The excellent oxidase-like performance of CuMnO2NFs was attributed to the synergistic effect of Cu and Mn in CuMnO2NFs and the unique nanoflower-like structure of CuMnO2NFs which provided a large number of active sites for oxidase-like reactions. When  the  AA  was  added  to  the  ox-TMB  solution,  AA  captured  two electrons from ox-TMB which turned ox-TMB (blue) into TMB (colorless), and the ascorbic acid (AA) was reduced to dehydroascorbic acid.

# 3.6. Detection of AA in fruit and vegetable juices


Based on the TMB-CuMnO2NFs catalytic reaction system, a detection system for AA in fruits and vegetables was established. Fruits and vegetables used for testing include: sour jujube, red chilli, shepherd ' s purse,  pomegranate,  kiwi,  hawthorn,  strawberry,  grape  and  tomato. First of all, CuMnO2NFs and TMB formed a stable coexisting solution of ox-TMB and CuMnO2NFs (blue color). Then, 30 μ L of each of vegetable juices and fruit juices was added to1mL of ox-TMB solution, and the absorbance value of the solution was measured at652nm. As can be seen from the Fig. 9 (A), compared with the absorbance value at652nm of  the  ox-TMB  solution  (blank  sample),  the  absorbance  value  of  the solution had the largest decrease in the presence of sour jujube, and the least decrease with absorbance at652nm was observed in the case of tomato. The reason for this phenomenon was the fact that AA concentration was maximum in the sour jujube, while it was minimum in tomato.  Thus,  the  content  of  AA  in  fruit  and  vegetable  juices  was determined by comparing the changes in absorbance values at652nm between the ox-TMB solution (blank sample) and the fruit and vegetable juice solutions, which also could be confirmed by the color changes of the fruit and vegetable juice solution. So far, a detection system for AA content in fruit and vegetable juice had been established based on TMBCuMnO2NFs.

# 3.7. The reproducibility of CuMnO2NFs


To further test the reproducibility of the colorimetric detection system, the TMB-CuMnO2NFs were tested for5cycles. As shown in Fig. S4, the  relevant  activity  of  the  TMB-CuMnO2NFs  colorimetric  detection system remained96.5% after5cycles. Compared with other reported detection  platforms  [107 -109],  TMB-CuMnO2maintained  excellent detection sensitivity after5cycles under optimal reaction conditions. The time stability of TMB-CuMnO2NFs colorimetric detection system was also tested.

couples.  When  the  TMB-CuMnO2NFs  catalytic  reaction  system  was combined with AA, ox-TMB (blue) was reduced to TMB (colorless) by AA. Based on this, a rapid colorimetric detection platform for AA concentration had been constructed. The detection range of TMB-CuMnO2NFs detection platform for AA was1 -105 µ M and the LOD was0.39 µ M. Due to the good sensitivity and reliability of TMB-CuMnO2NFs system for detection of AA, the TMB-CuMnO2NFs colorimetric detection platform was applied to the detection of AA content in fruit and vegetable juices. Through the spike-and-recovery experiment, the detection results of AA concentration in fruit and vegetable juices by TMB-CuMnO2NFs colorimetric detection were close to the results obtained by HPLC, which indicated  that  TMB-CuMnO2NFs  colorimetric  detection  method  was effective  and  accurate  for  AA.  The  TMB-CuMnO2NFs  colorimetric detection platform for AA content in fruit and vegetable juices based on the CuMnO2NFs was reliable and accurate. In conclusion, the simple preparation method of CuMnO2NFs enabled Cu-based artificial enzymes to possess oxidase-like properties, which is an extension of the performance of Cu-based artificial enzymes and a beneficial attempt to obtain oxidase-like properties of Cu-based artificial enzymes. According to this method, Cu element can be complexed with other oxidase-like metals to obtain Cu-based oxidases with more catalytic properties.
